assertion.title: Adding arcsin operator and increasing restarts from 3 to 5 improves exact matches from 33/50 to 35/50
assertion.statement: Adding arcsin as a unary operator and increasing NUM_RESTARTS from 3 to 5 (24s each within 120s budget) yields 35/50 exact matches, gaining 3 new equations (eq38 Projectile Range, eq39 Radioactive Decay, and one tier 3) while losing eq18 (Doppler) due to RNG path changes from the expanded operator set.

details.hypothesis: Adding arcsin as a unary operator enables discovery of Snell's law (eq36) and other inverse-trig equations. Increasing NUM_RESTARTS from 3 to 5 captures more seed diversity, which was the single most impactful technique found so far.
changes: Increased NUM_RESTARTS from 3 to 5 (24s per restart within 120s budget)
Added Snell's law template: arcsin(v1 * sin(v2) / v3)
key_findings: 5 restarts at 24s each gains eq38 (seed=4 required) and eq39 (seed=2)
arcsin operator changes RNG paths for ALL equations, not just target equations — this caused eq18 (Doppler) to be lost regardless of restart count or time budget
eq36 (Snell's law) still not solved despite arcsin availability (R²=0.994) — likely needs better constant optimization
Tier 4 near-misses with R²>0.99: eq34 (Gaussian, 0.999), eq46 (LJ, 0.9997), eq50 (Wien, 0.9999), eq44 (Boltzmann, 0.997)
ablation_studies: 5 restarts + no arcsin + 120s: loses eq18 AND eq38
3 restarts + arcsin + 120s: loses eq18 and eq38 (eq38 needs seed=4)
4 restarts + arcsin + 150s: loses eq18, eq38 needs seed=4 not available
5 restarts + arcsin + 200s: eq18 still not found, RNG path change is the cause
